As it requires some effort to reach, this is a secluded beach offering scenic views of the Golden Gate Bridge and the Marin Headlands. It can be reached by hiking along the Battery to Bluffs Trail at the Presidio of San Francisco. This trail is off the main trail known as the California Coastal trail. You will need to leave the trail and descend down a staircase before reaching the shore. However, once here, the scene is pretty spectacular with waves crashing in on the rock-strewn beach with great views of the Golden Gate Bridge in the distance. Definitely worth the effort to get here and it was my favorite part of the hike that began at the Golden Gate Welcome Center and ended at Baker's Beach. We were the only ones on the beach that Saturday afternoon. Although it was sunny, it was cool and windy. Warmer weather may bring out more beach goers I suspect.
